copy.caption: Riding thermals in lazy circles high above everything else.
copy.shortDescription: Vultures barely need to flap — they spread their huge wings and ride rising columns of warm air called thermals, circling for hours without breaking a sweat. They have bald heads because feathers would get very messy doing what vultures do for a living. Fun fact: A vulture's stomach acid is strong enough to destroy anthrax and botulism bacteria that would kill most other animals.
